atten3.ptxt: This section describes the standard CAN values for all CAN related components.

step1.ptxt: JUNCTION CONNECTOR (NO. 1 CAN J/C, NO. 2 CAN J/C)

step2.ptxt: No. 1 CAN J/C

entry.ptxt: No. 1 CAN J/C connectors ("A" side, with earth terminal)
No. 1 CAN J/C connectors ("B" side, with earth terminal)

step2.ptxt: *: with VSC
No. 2 CAN J/C (FRONT OF THE VEHICLE)
No. 2 CAN J/C (REAR OF THE VEHICLE)
The connection diagram of the components which are connected to the CAN J/C
Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below.

entry.ptxt: HIGH-level CAN bus wire - LOW-level CAN bus wire
HIGH-level CAN bus wire - Battery positive
LOW-level CAN bus wire - Battery positive

step2.ptxt: Disconnect the A25 connector from the skid control ECU.
Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below.

entry.ptxt: HIGH-level CAN bus wire - LOW-level CAN bus wire
HIGH-level CAN bus wire - Battery positive
LOW-level CAN bus wire - Battery positive

step2.ptxt: Check the harness side connector (E38) of the air conditioning amplifier.
